meta.title: Anastasia Vojkic · Software Developer
meta.description: Idea → project → analysis → constraints → strategy → creativity → build → challenge the system. TypeScript developer, back-end oriented, driven by a vision: technical, usage, context.

intro.title: Profile
intro.text1:
I hold a professional degree in Application Design and Development.

I’m particularly interested in software architecture, data modeling, and the way technical decisions shape real-world usage.

vision.title: Approach
vision.text:
I see myself primarily as a back-end developer. I’ve also worked on the front-end, which gives me a broader understanding of how applications actually come together.

In a team, I naturally gravitate towards back-end work. When working more independently, I take ownership of the development and adapt my role depending on the context.

A back-end is never invisible. It always shows through the limits of the interface.
That’s why I think from the end user’s perspective, while keeping in mind the more creative ones… the ones who try to push or break the system.

I approach projects with an analytical mindset and a strong personal standard that drives me to dig deeper until everything makes sense.
This is reflected in the attention I give to data modeling and application architecture.

goals.title: Goals
goals.text1: Contribute to meaningful and innovative projects.
goals.text2: Help companies design, improve, and evolve their tools.
